title: luzula campestris var. crinita
intro: Luzula campestris var. crinita, also known as hairy woodrush, is a perennial plant species within the sedge family. The hairy woodrush is used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. Its medicinal properties are believed to provide health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the hairy woodrush is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve.
